title: The wrong coat
summary: Two passengers on the same train picked up the same brown coat. Only one of them was supposed to.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Excuse me. I think you have my coat.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Could you check the inside pocket?

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Because my mother's photo is in there. She wrote my name on the back.

body.speaker: Quan
body.text: …oh. There is a photo. And a name. Linh.

body.speaker: Quan
body.text: I am very sorry. I picked it up on the train. They look the same. Mine is also brown.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: It is okay. Mistakes happen. Where is your coat?

body.speaker: Quan
body.text: I do not know. I thought this was mine.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Then someone else has yours. Let us check the train office.

body.speaker: Quan
body.text: Are you not in a hurry?

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: A little. But I can wait twenty minutes.

body.speaker: Quan
body.text: Why are you doing this for me?

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Last year, somebody helped me find my bag. They missed their train, but they helped me. I remember.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Come on. The office is this way. What was in your coat?

body.speaker: Quan
body.text: My grandfather's watch. He gave it to me last month.

body.speaker: Linh
body.text: Then we have to find it. Quickly.

annotations.meaningVi: đã lỡ chuyến tàu của họ

grammarNotes.title: 'Could you...?' for polite requests
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Could you + động từ?' là cách lịch sự để yêu cầu ai đó làm gì. Trong bài: 'Could you check the inside pocket?' = 'Anh có thể kiểm tra túi trong giúp tôi được không?' Nhẹ nhàng hơn 'Can you...?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Could you + verb?' is a polite way to ask someone to do something. From the dialogue: 'Could you check the inside pocket?' This is softer than 'Can you check…?'

translationVi:
Linh: Xin lỗi. Tôi nghĩ anh đang cầm áo khoác của tôi.
Quân: Hả? Đây là áo của tôi mà.
Linh: Anh có thể kiểm tra túi bên trong không?
Quân: Tại sao?
Linh: Vì ảnh của mẹ tôi nằm trong đó. Bà ấy viết tên tôi ở mặt sau.
Quân: …à. Có một tấm ảnh. Và một cái tên. Linh.
Linh: Đó là tôi.
Quân: Tôi xin lỗi. Tôi cầm áo này trên tàu. Hai cái nhìn giống hệt nhau. Áo của tôi cũng màu nâu.
Linh: Không sao. Ai cũng có lúc nhầm. Áo của anh đâu rồi?
Quân: Tôi không biết. Tôi cứ tưởng đây là của tôi.
Linh: Vậy thì có người khác đang giữ áo của anh. Mình ra văn phòng nhà tàu xem nhé.
Quân: Cô không vội à?
Linh: Hơi vội một chút. Nhưng tôi có thể chờ hai mươi phút.
Quân: Sao cô lại giúp tôi như vậy?
Linh: Năm ngoái có người giúp tôi tìm cái túi. Người đó lỡ tàu, nhưng vẫn giúp tôi. Tôi nhớ.
Quân: Cảm ơn cô. Thật sự cảm ơn.
Linh: Đi nào. Văn phòng phía này. Trong áo anh có gì?
Quân: Đồng hồ của ông tôi. Tháng trước ông cho tôi.
Linh: Vậy thì chúng ta phải tìm nó. Nhanh lên.

questions.prompt: Why does Linh think Quan has her coat?
options: Her mother's photo with her name is in the inside pocket.
The coat is a different colour.
questions.explanation: 'My mother's photo is in there. She wrote my name on the back.'

questions.prompt: Why did Quan take the wrong coat?
options: The two coats look the same — both brown.
There was no other coat on the train.
questions.explanation: 'They look the same. Mine is also brown.'
questions.hint: Quan explains it after Linh says the photo is hers.

questions.prompt: What does Linh suggest they do?
questions.hint: Linh's response after Quan says he does not know where his coat is.

questions.prompt: Why does Linh agree to wait, even though she is in a hurry?
options: Last year, somebody helped her find her bag and missed their train.
She wants to find her own coat first.
questions.explanation: 'Last year, somebody helped me find my bag. They missed their train, but they helped me. I remember.'
questions.hint: Linh's reply to 'Why are you doing this for me?'

questions.prompt: What is in Quan's coat that he wants to find?
questions.explanation: 'My grandfather's watch. He gave it to me last month.'
questions.hint: Quan's last turn before Linh says 'we have to find it.'

questions.prompt: When did Quan receive the watch?
questions.explanation: 'He gave it to me last month.'
questions.hint: Quan tells Linh when describing the watch.

options: 'It is okay. Anyone could make this mistake.'
questions.explanation: Linh is reassuring Quan; she is saying not to worry.
questions.hint: Linh's reply after Quan says sorry.

questions.prompt: What kind of person is Linh?
options: Kind — she gives her time to help a stranger because someone once helped her.
Angry — she wants Quan to pay her.
Shy — she does not want to talk.
Tired — she just wants to go home.
questions.explanation: She offers to wait twenty minutes to help find Quan's coat.
questions.hint: Why does she stop to help?

questions.prompt: What does Quan's tone change to by the end?
questions.explanation: 'Thank you. Really.' He starts defensive and becomes thankful.
questions.hint: Compare 'This is my coat' to 'Thank you. Really.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: A small kindness received once can be passed on years later.
People should always check their pockets.
Brown coats are a bad idea.
questions.explanation: Linh helps Quan because someone helped her last year — she is repeating the kindness.
questions.hint: Look at Linh's reason for staying.

blanks.explanation: Linh thinks Quan has her coat.
She asks him to check the inside pocket.
Her mother wrote the name on the back of the photo.
They will check the train office.
The person who helped her missed their train.
His grandfather's watch was in the coat.
Linh says they must find it quickly.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Linh helps Quan because someone once helped her. Has a small kind action you received from a stranger ever pushed you to do something similar for someone else? What did it cost you, and was it worth it?
